rationale: The agent correctly found PR #2, read the diff, and read both branch and main files. It produced a review in its final_answer requesting changes based on the incomplete implementation (dead helper function, TODO comment only, no actual --token wiring). However, the agent failed two key requirements of the expected behavior: (1) It never submitted a pull_request_review_write tool call — no review was actually posted to the PR. (2) The review body does not mention connection-string injection (the f-string in build_uri_with_token lacks URL-encoding of the token, so tokens containing @/?#/ would break URI parsing) or token-in-argv (the --token flag would expose the secret via ps/proc/cmdline). The agent focused entirely on the incomplete wiring rather than the security flaws in the code that was actually added. The docs/test/help desynchronization was also not mentioned. Since the review was never submitted and the required security findings were absent, the task is a failure.
